capture.cameraPose: Desktop browser viewport capture after raising in-scene EHR, dialogue, and input-evidence panels for clearer visual QA
capture.captureCommand: Chrome DevTools MCP evaluate_script reload; wait_for Trace Actions; take_screenshot fullPage

notes: The station still reads as a coarse runtime preview with primitive actors and sparse ED equipment; raised wall-style text panels improve reviewability but do not create a realistic high-pressure clinical environment.
Actors remain capsule/head placeholders with no clothing, hand meshes, facial expression, pain response, rigging, or realistic medical equipment density.
The Simulated EHR and Live Dialogue in-scene panels are more readable after being raised and shifted away from the bed plane; the desktop side panel remains readable and internally scrollable.
Trace buttons and Full VR entry remain visible in desktop preview, but the screenshot still cannot prove headset entry, controller select, hand tracking, locomotion, or latency.

Occlusion is reduced for EHR/dialogue text, but the in-scene Input Evidence panel still overlaps the primitive central actor from this camera pose; spatial UI placement and UIKitML/IWSDK text remain future work.
This is desktop Chrome DevTools visual iteration evidence only; it is not physical Quest proof, not immersive WebXR proof, not in-headset readability proof, and not production runtime readiness.
